posts.title: Why Nigeria's marginal fields are the smart play in 2026
posts.author: Onterra Insights
posts.excerpt: Smaller, well-understood assets are where disciplined operators are creating value. Here is how a marginal field becomes a producing asset.
posts.body:
Marginal fields have moved from being an afterthought to one of the most attractive entry points in the Nigerian upstream. They are smaller, more defined, and reachable for independents who can move quickly and manage cost.

The opportunity

What makes a marginal field work is not the size of the resource, it is the discipline of the operator and the completeness of the team behind it. A field that has been mapped, evaluated and developed by people who understand the whole system can reach first oil far faster than a larger asset run by a fragmented group.

What it takes

  • A clean, fundable acquisition structure
  • A credible subsurface story
  • A development plan sequenced toward first oil
  • The manpower and partners to execute on the ground

That combination, knowledge of the system plus people who can act on it, is exactly what turns a marginal field from a licence on paper into flowing barrels.

posts.title: From purchase to first oil: the six stages that matter
posts.author: Onterra Insights
posts.excerpt: A field's journey is a chain, and it only delivers if every link holds. We break down the path we manage end to end.
posts.body:
Bringing a field online is a sequence, and the value is lost wherever the chain breaks. We manage all six stages as one continuous journey.

1. Acquisition & purchase

Structuring and facilitating a clean entry into the asset.

2. Subsurface evaluation

Re-interpreting the reservoir and defining the resource with confidence.

3. Field development planning

Engineering the facilities, wells and contracting strategy.

4. Execution & well delivery

Drilling, completions, wireline and coiled tubing.

5. First oil

Commissioning and bringing the field online.

6. Production & market delivery

Optimising production and getting crude to market. One accountable partner across the whole arc removes the gaps where projects usually stall.

posts.title: Coiled tubing and wireline: keeping mature wells alive
posts.author: Onterra Insights
posts.excerpt: Intervention is where lost barrels are recovered. A look at how coiled tubing and wireline services sustain production.
posts.body:
The difference between a declining well and a productive one is often a well-timed intervention. Coiled tubing and wireline services are the tools that recover barrels that would otherwise be left in the ground.

Coiled tubing

Clean-outs, stimulation, nitrogen lifting and servicing, restoring flow and extending the productive life of a well.

Wireline

Slickline and electric-line operations for logging, perforation, intervention and data acquisition across the life of the well.

Run together by a coordinated team, these services turn well integrity from a risk into an advantage.

posts.title: The real edge: understanding the whole system
posts.author: Onterra Insights
posts.excerpt: Regulatory, commercial, subsurface, surface, the advantage is seeing how every piece connects, not just one of them.
posts.body:
Most teams know a slice of the upstream. The advantage that actually moves a project is seeing the whole picture, and having the people to act on it.

One understanding, four domains

  • Regulatory, the approvals and compliance pathway
  • Commercial, how the deal and the economics are structured
  • Subsurface, what is really in the reservoir
  • Surface, the facilities and operations that bring it up

When one partner holds all four, decisions are faster, cleaner and far more likely to reach first oil.
